Ecclesiastical Offices (Terms of Service) Measure 2009 (Appointed Day No. 3) Instrument 2010
items.description: This Instrument brings into force, on 27th September 2010, subsections (1), (2) and (3) of section 11 of the Ecclesiastical Offices (Terms of Service) Measure 2009. The remaining provisions of the Measure are to be brought into force on a day or days yet to be appointed.

items.title: The Vacancies in Suffragan Sees and Other Ecclesiastical Offices Measure 2010 (Appointed Day No. 3) Instrument 2010
items.description: This Instrument brings into force, on 1st January 2011, section 2 of the Vacancies in Suffragan Sees and Other Ecclesiastical Offices Measure 2010. The  provisions commenced provide for the exercise by 'the relevant bishop' of the Crown's right of presentation to an ecclesiastical office which is vacant during a vacancy in a diocesan see and to which the right of presentataion would, but for the vacancy in see, have been exercisable by the diocesan bishop.

items.title: The Crown Benefices (Parish Representatives) Measure 2010 (Appointed Day) Instrument 2010
items.description: This Instrument brings into force, on 1st January 2011, the whole of the Crown Benefices (Parish Representatives) Measure 2010. The  Measure makes provision for the appointment of lay parish representatives to approve the selection of incumbents of benefices that are in the patronage of the Crown  (including Lord Chancellor's patronage), the Duchy of Lancaster and the Duchy of Cornwall.

items.title: The Ecclesiastical Offices (Terms of Service) Measure 2009 (Appointed Day No. 4) Instrument 2010
items.description: This Instrument brings into force, on 31st January 2011, the remaining provisions of the Ecclesiastical Offices (Terms of Service)Measure 2009, with the exception of paragraphs 6, 10, 12 and 13 of Schedule 2.

items.title: Beverley Freemen Act 2010
items.description: An Act to re-state and extend the categories of person entitled by birthright to be enrolled as a Freeman of Beverley; to extend the area within which entitlement to be a Pasture Freeman may arise; to modify legislation relating to such entitlement; and for related purposes.

items.title: Bournemouth Borough Council Act 2010
items.description: An Act to confer powers on Bournemouth Borough Council for the better control of street trading in the borough of Bournemouth.

items.title: Manchester City Council Act 2010
items.description: An Act to confer powers on Manchester City Council for the better control of street trading in the city of Manchester.

items.title: Kent County Council (Filming on Highways) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to confer powers on Kent County Council in relation to filming on highways; and for related purposes.

items.title: Allhallows Staining Church Act 2010
items.description: An act to remove certain restrictions relating to the use of land comprising the former church of Allhallows Staining, its churchyard, and other adjoining land in the City of London; to make provision for the removal of any human remains from the land and to enable its use for other purposes; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Video Recordings Act 2010
items.description: An Act to repeal and revive provisions of the Video Recordings Act 1984.

items.title: Third Parties (Rights against Insurers) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision about the rights of third parties against insurers of liabilities to third parties in the case where the insured is insolvent, and in certain other cases.

items.title: Cluster Munitions (Prohibitions) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision for giving effect to the Convention on Cluster Munitions.

items.title: Appropriation (No. 2) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to Appropriate the supply authorised in this Session of Parliament for the service of the year ending with 31 March 2011.

items.title: Finance Act 2010
items.description: An Act to grant certain duties, to alter other duties, and to amend the law relating to the National Debt and the Public Revenue, and to make further provision in connection with finance.

items.title: Anti-Slavery Day Act 2010
items.description: An Act to introduce a national day to raise awareness of the need to eradicate all forms of slavery, human trafficking and exploitation; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Equality Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision to require Ministers of the Crown and others when making strategic decisions about the exercise of their functions to have regard to the desirability of reducing socio-economic inequalities; to reform and harmonise equality law and restate the greater part of the enactments relating to discrimination and harassment related to certain personal characteristics; to enable certain employers to be required to publish information about the differences in pay between male and female employees; to prohibit victimisation in certain circumstances; to require the exercise of certain functions to be with regard to the need to eliminate discrimination and other prohibited conduct; to enable duties to be imposed in relation to the exercise of public procurement functions; to increase equality of opportunity; to amend the law relating to rights and responsibilities in family relationships; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Northern Ireland Assembly Members Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision relating to salaries, allowances and pensions for members of the Northern Ireland Assembly.

items.title: Crime and Security Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision about police powers of stop and search; about the taking, retention, destruction and use of evidential material; for the protection of victims of domestic violence; about injunctions in respect of gang-related violence; about anti-social behaviour orders; about the private security industry; about possession and use of electronic communications devices in prison; about air weapons; for the compensation of victims of overseas terrorism; about licensing the sale and supply of alcohol; about searches in relation to persons subject to control orders; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Personal Care at Home Act 2010 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to amend section 15 of the Community Care (Delayed Discharges etc.) Act 2003 so as to remove the restriction on the period for which personal care may be provided free of charge to persons living at home; and to make consequential provision.

items.title: Mortgage Repossessions (Protection of Tenants etc) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to protect persons whose tenancies are not binding on mortgagees and to require mortgagees to give notice of the proposed execution of possession orders.

items.title: Terrorist Asset-Freezing (Temporary Provisions) Act 2010 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision for the temporary validity of certain Orders in Council imposing financial restrictions on, and in relation to, persons suspected of involvement in terrorist activity; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Sunbeds (Regulation) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision about the use or supply of tanning devices that use artificial ultra-violet radiation; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Sustainable Communities Act 2007 (Amendment) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to amend the Sustainable Communities Act 2007.

items.title: Debt Relief (Developing Countries) Act 2010
items.description: Make provision for or in connection with the relief of debts of certain developing countries.

items.title: Bribery Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision about offences relating to bribery; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Digital Economy Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision about the functions of the Office of Communications; to make provision about the online infringement of copyright and about penalties for infringement of copyright and performers’ rights; to make provision about internet domain registries; to make provision about the functions of the Channel Four Television Corporation; to make provision about the regulation of television and radio services; to make provision about the regulation of the use of the electromagnetic spectrum; to amend the Video Recordings Act 1984; to make provision about public lending right in relation to electronic publications; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Constitutional Reform and Governance Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision relating to the civil service of the State; to make provision in relation to section 3 of the Act of Settlement; to make provision relating to the ratification of treaties; to make provision relating to the counting of votes in parliamentary elections; to amend the Parliamentary Standards Act 2009 and the European Parliament (Pay and Pensions) Act 1979 and to make provision relating to pensions for members of the House of Commons, Ministers and other office holders; to make provision for treating members of the House of Commons and members of the House of Lords as resident, ordinarily resident and domiciled in the United Kingdom for taxation purposes; to amend the Government Resources and Accounts Act 2000 and to make corresponding provision in relation to Wales; to amend the Public Records Act 1958 and the Freedom of Information Act 2000.

items.title: Children, Schools and Families Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision about children with disabilities or special educational needs, school and other education, and governing bodies’ powers; to make provision amending the Education Acts; to make provision about Local Safeguarding Children Boards; and to make provision about publication of information relating to family proceedings.

items.title: Energy Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision relating to the demonstration, assessment and use of carbon capture and storage technology; to make provision about reports on decarbonisation of electricity generation and development and use of carbon capture and storage technology; to make provision for requiring benefits to be provided by holders of gas or electricity supply licences; to make provision about functions of the Gas and Electricity Markets Authority; to make provision about general duties of the Secretary of State in relation to gas and electricity markets; to make provision about electricity generation licences; to make provision about persons authorised to supply gas or electricity; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Financial Services Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision amending the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000, including provision about financial education, and other provision about financial services and markets; and to make provision for the administration of court funds by the Director of Savings.

items.title: Flood and Water Management Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision about water, including provision about the management of risks in connection with flooding and coastal erosion.

items.title: Fiscal Responsibility Act 2010 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision for and in connection with the imposition of duties for securing sound public finances.

items.title: Appropriation (No. 3) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to Authorise the use of resources for the service of the year ending with 31 March 2011 and to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the year ending with 31 March 2011; to appropriate the supply authorised in this Session of Parliament for the service of the year ending with 31 March 2011; and to repeal certain Consolidated Fund and Appropriation Acts.

items.title: Finance (No. 2) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to grant certain duties, to alter other duties, and to amend the law relating to the National Debt and the Public Revenue, and to make further provision in connection with finance.

items.title: Academies Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision about Academies.

items.title: Finance (No. 3) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to grant certain duties, to alter other duties, and to amend the law relating to the National Debt and the Public Revenue, and to make further provision in connection with finance.

items.title: Equitable Life (Payments) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to provide finance for payments in cases where persons have been adversely affected by maladministration in the regulation before December 2001 of the Equitable Life Assurance Society; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Local Government Act 2010
items.description: An Act to prevent the implementation of existing proposals made for the purposes of Part 1 of the Local Government and Public Involvement in Health Act 2007.

items.title: Savings Accounts and Health in Pregnancy Grant Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision about eligibility for a child trust fund; to repeal the Saving Gateway Accounts Act 2009; to make provision about entitlement to health in pregnancy grant; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Superannuation Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision for and in connection with limiting the value of the benefits which may be provided under so much of any scheme under section 1 of the Superannuation Act 1972 as provides by virtue of section 2(2) of that Act for benefits to be provided by way of compensation to or in respect of persons who suffer loss of office or employment; and to make provision about the procedure for modifying such a scheme.

items.title: Terrorist Asset-Freezing etc. Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision for imposing financial restrictions on, and in relation to, certain persons believed or suspected to be, or to have been, involved in terrorist activities; to amend Schedule 7 to the Counter-Terrorism Act 2008; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Consolidated Fund Act 2010
items.description: An Act to authorise the use of resources for the service of the year ending with 31 March 2011 and to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the year ending with 31 March 2011; and to authorise the use of resources for the year ending with 31 March 2012 and the issue of sums out of the Consolidated Fund for the year ending with 31 March 2012.

items.title: Corporation Tax Act 2010
items.description: An Act to restate, with minor changes, certain enactments relating to corporation tax and certain enactments relating to company distributions; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Identity Documents Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision for and in connection with the repeal of the Identity Cards Act 2006.

items.title: Loans to Ireland Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision in connection with the making of loans to Ireland by the United Kingdom.

items.title: Appropriation Act 2010
items.description: An Act to authorise the use of resources for the service of the years ending with 31 March 2009 and 31 March 2010 and to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the year ending with 31 March 2010; and to appropriate the supply authorised in this Session of Parliament for the service of the years ending with 31 March 2009 and 31 March 2010.

items.title: Marriage (Wales) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to enable persons to be married in a place of worship in a parish in the Church in Wales with which they have a qualifying connection; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Co-operative and Community Benefit Societies and Credit Unions Act 2010
items.description: An Act to make provision for societies to be registered as co-operative or community benefit societies and to re-name the Industrial and Provident Societies Acts; to apply to registered societies the provisions relating to directors disqualification and to make provision for the application of certain other enactments relating to companies; to confer power to make provision for credit unions corresponding to any provision applying to building societies; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Taxation (International and Other Provisions) Act 2010
items.description: An Act to restate, with minor changes, certain enactments relating to tax; to make provision for purposes connected with the restatement of enactments by other tax law rewrite Acts; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Child Poverty Act 2010
items.description: An Act to set targets relating to the eradication of child poverty, and to make other provision about child poverty.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Levy Ceiling – Earnings Percentage Increase) Order 2010
items.description: This Order specifies that, for the purposes of section 178(3)(a) of the Pensions Act 2004 (c.35) (“the Act”), the earnings percentage for the period from 1st August 2008 to 31st July 2009 (“the review period”) is 0.9%.

items.title: The Pensions Act 2008 (Commencement No. 5) Order 2010
items.description: This Order brings into force sections 75 (in so far as not already in force) and 76 of, and Schedule 1 (in so far as not already in force) to, the Pensions Act 2008 (c.30) (“the Act”) on 5th July 2010. Those provisions establish the National Employment Savings Trust Corporation and make provision for its constitution and functions. This Order also brings into force section 99 of the Act which is an interpretation provision.

items.title: The Lewisham Hospital National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2010
items.description: This Order amends the Lewisham Hospital National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1993 (“the Establishment Order”), which established the Lewisham Hospital National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The National Health Service (Direct Payments) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations enable the Secretary of State for Health to make pilot schemes in accordance with which direct payments may be made to secure the provision of certain health services under the National Health Service Act 2006 or the Mental Health Act 1983.

items.title: The Air Quality Standards Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations implement the following Directives—

items.title: The Education (Independent Educational Provision in England) (Inspection Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations are being made to correct a defect in the Education (Independent Educational Provision in England) (Inspection Fees) Regulations 2009 (S.I. 2009/1607) (“the 2009 Regulations”). The 2009 Regulations make provision about fees to be paid by independent educational institutions under Chapter 1 of Part 4 of the Education and Skills Act 2008. The Regulations are concerned with independent educational institutions that are independent schools within the meaning in section 463 of the Education Act 1996 but are not Academies, city technology colleges or city colleges for the technology of the arts. The fees are fees for inspections by Her Majesty’s Chief Inspector of Education, Children’s Services and Skills (“the Chief Inspector”).

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Off Road Events) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Off Road Events) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/1371) (“the 1995 Regulations”). These amendments apply in relation to motoring events taking place wholly or partly in England in respect of which an application for authorisation under the 1995 Regulations is made on or after the coming into force of these Regulations. Where an event takes place partly in England and partly elsewhere, these Regulations apply only to the part taking place in England.

items.title: The Identification and Traceability of Explosives Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement, as regards Great Britain, Commission Directive 2008/43/EC setting up, pursuant to Council Directive 93/15/EC, a system for the identification and traceability of explosives for civil uses (“the 2008 Directive”). The Regulations impose requirements with respect to the marking of explosives with a unique identification and will come into force on 5th April 2012.

items.title: The Motor Vehicle (Competitions and Trials) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicle (Competitions and Trials) Regulations 1969 (S.I. 1969/414). These amendments apply in relation to events taking place wholly or partly in England in respect of which an application for authorisation under the 1969 Regulations is made on or after the coming into force of these Regulations. Where an event takes place partly in England and partly elsewhere, the amendments apply only to the part taking place in England.

items.title: The School Information (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the School Information (England) Regulations 2008 and come into force on 1 September 2010.

items.title: The Planning Act 2008 (Commencement No.4 and Saving) Order 2010
items.description: Article 2 of this Order brings into force, on 1st March 2010, section 36 of and Schedule 2 to the Planning Act 2008 (“the Act”) in relation to the United Kingdom (amendments consequential on the development consent regime).

items.title: The Consumer Credit ( EU Directive) Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement Directive 2008/48/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on credit agreements for consumers (OJ No L133, 22.5.2008, p66) (“the Directive”) except for article 4, paragraphs 1 to 5 of article 5, article 10 and article 19.

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Total Charge for Credit) Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement article 19 of and Annex 1 to Directive 2008/48/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on credit agreements for consumers (OJ No L133, 22.5.2008, p66) (“the Directive”). They set out the basis on which the annual percentage rate of charge (APR) and the total charge for credit must be calculated for the purpose of an actual or prospective regulated consumer credit agreement.

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Advertisements) Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement article 4 of Directive 2008/48/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on credit agreements for consumers (OJ No L133, 22.5.2008, p66) (“the Directive”). They impose requirements concerning the form and content of advertisements that relate to the provision of credit. By virtue of regulation 11, these Regulations do not apply in relation to advertisements for loans of goods to businesses, to financial promotions or to consumer credit agreements secured on land. The Consumer Credit (Advertisements) Regulations 2004 (“the 2004 Regulations”) will continue to apply to consumer credit agreements secured on land only.

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Disclosure of Information) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations implement article 5.1 to 5.5, and article 6.4 of Directive 2008/48/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on credit agreements for consumers (OJ No. L133, 22.5.2008, p.66) They also implement the pre-contractual information requirements in articles 3.1 to 3.3 and 5.2 of Directive 2002/65/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council concerning the distance marketing of consumer financial services and amending Council Directive 90/61/EEC and Directives 97/7/EC and 98/27/EC in their application to consumer credit agreements (OJ No.L271, 9.10.2002, p.16.).

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Agreements) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations implement article 10 of Directive 2008/48/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on credit agreements for consumers (OJ No. L133, 22.5.2008, p.66) (“the Directive”) and impose requirements about what information has to be included in a regulated consumer credit agreement.

items.title: The Infrastructure Planning (Interested Parties) Regulations 2010
items.description: The Planning Act 2008 (c.29) establishes the Infrastructure Planning Commission (“IPC”) and provides for the granting of development consent for certain types of nationally significant infrastructure projects. Chapter 4 of Part 6 of the Act sets out the procedure for examining applications for development consent. This procedure provides that “interested parties”, among other things, can notify the IPC of a wish to be heard at an open-floor hearing and are entitled to make oral representations about the application at the open-floor hearing.

items.title: The Chief Executive of Skills Funding (Strategy for Birmingham City Region) Order 2010
items.description: This Order specifies, for the purposes of section 112 of the Apprenticeships, Skills, Children and Learning Act 2009, the Birmingham City Region Employment and Skills Board as the body responsible for formulating and keeping under review a strategy for the area of Birmingham City Region (article 2).

items.title: The A43 Trunk Road (M40 Junction 10 to M1 Junction 15a) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A1 Trunk Road (Stamford to Grantham) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A1 Trunk Road (Tickencote to Tinwell) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A46 Trunk Road (Leicester to Thorpe on the Hill) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic in Laybys) Order 2010
The Infrastructure Planning (Examination Procedure) Rules 2010
items.description: The Planning Act 2008 (“the Act) establishes the Infrastructure Planning Commission (“the IPC”) and provides for the granting of development consent for certain types of nationally significant infrastructure projects. Part 6 of the Act sets out the procedure for examining the majority of applications for development consent.

items.title: The M25 Motorway (Junctions 19 – 21A) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M4 Motorway (Junction 7, Westbound Exit Slip Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A1(M) Motorway (Junction 1, Southbound Exit Slip Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Parliamentary Standards Act 2009 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2010
items.description: This Order brings into force provisions of the Parliamentary Standards Act 2009 (“the 2009 Act”) relating to MPs’ salaries and to the MPs’ allowances scheme under which the Independent Parliamentary Standards Authority (“the IPSA”) is to pay allowances to Members of Parliament.

items.title: The M3 Motorway (Junctions 8 - 9) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Guardian’s Allowance Up-rating Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations supersede the Guardian’s Allowance Up-rating Regulations 2009 (S.I. 2009/810).

items.title: The A27 Trunk Road (South of Cophall Roundabout – East of Pevensey Bypass Roundabout) (Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2010
The A31 Trunk Road (Ringwood Road, St Leonards) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A21 Trunk Road (John’s Cross – Marley Lane) (Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2010
The M2 Motorway (Junctions 1 - 3) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Infrastructure Planning (Compulsory Acquisition) Regulations 2010
items.description: The Planning Act 2008 (“the Act”) establishes the Infrastructure Planning Commission and provides for the granting of development consent for certain types of nationally significant infrastructure projects. Part 6 of the Act sets out the procedure for examining applications for development consent. Part 7 contains provisions in respect of what can be included in an order granting development consent. Among other things, an order granting development consent can authorise the compulsory acquisition of land.

items.title: The A1(M) Motorway (Junction 44, Bramham Crossroads) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M62 Motorway (Junction 24, Ainley Top) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A1 Trunk Road (Tweedmouthmoor to Letham Shank) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) (No.2) Order 2010
The A1(M) Motorway (Junction 56 to Junction 57) and the A66(M) Motorway (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) (No.2) Order 2010
The A34 Trunk Road (Gore Hill, Northbound Carriageway) (Lay-By Closure and Restriction on Use of Offside Lane) (Experimental) Order 2010
The M27 Motorway (Junction 12, Eastbound) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A2 Trunk Road (Upper Harbledown – Bridge, Coastbound) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A21 Trunk Road (John’s Cross – Ebden’s Hill) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A38 Trunk Road (Carminow to Colesloggett, Near Bodmin, Cornwall) (Temporary Prohibition and Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The M62 Motorway (Junctions 7-6 Westbound Carriageway), the M57 Motorway (Junctions 2-1 Southbound Carriageway) and Link and Slip Roads (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Infrastructure Planning (Miscellaneous Prescribed Provisions) Regulations 2010
items.description: The Planning Act 2008 (c.29) establishes the Infrastructure Planning Commission and provides for the granting of development consent for certain types of nationally significant infrastructure projects.

items.title: The Planning (Hazardous Substances) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: The Planning (Hazardous Substances) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2009 (“the 2009 Regulations”) implemented, in relation to England, the land use planning provisions of Directive 96/82/EC on the control of major accident hazards involving dangerous substances (O.J. No. L. 10, 14.1.1997, p.13) (the Seveso II Directive), as amended by Council Directive 2003/105/EC (O.J. No. L. 345 31.12.2003, p.97) (the 2003 Directive).

items.title: The Whole of Government Accounts (Designation of Bodies) Order 2010
items.description: This Order designates the bodies listed in the Schedule in relation to the financial year ending on 31st March 2010 for the purposes of the Government Resources and Accounts Act 2000. The effect of designation is that these bodies are required to prepare and present to the Treasury such financial information in relation to that financial year as the Treasury require to enable them to prepare Whole of Government Accounts.

items.title: The A1(M) Motorway and the A1 Trunk Road (Dishforth Interchange to Leeming Bar Interchange) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M5 Motorway (Junctions 14-17) (Temporary Prohibition and Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The M6 Motorway (Junctions 39-41, Northbound and Southbound Carriageways) (Temporary Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The Additional Paternity Leave Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations implement the powers in the Work and Families Act 2006 to introduce a new entitlement for employees who are fathers or partners of mothers or adopters to take additional paternity leave in the first year of their child’s life or the first year after the child’s placement for adoption. The relevant provisions are incorporated by that Act into the Employment Rights Act 1996 (“the 1996 Act”).

items.title: The Additional Statutory Paternity Pay (General) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations implement the powers in the Work and Families Act 2006 (c. 18) (“the Act”) to introduce a new entitlement for employees who are fathers or who are the partners of mothers or adopters to receive a new statutory payment of additional statutory paternity pay from their employers. The relevant provisions are incorporated by the Act into the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (c. 4) (“the 1992 Act”).

items.title: The Additional Statutory Paternity Pay (Adoptions from Overseas) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations, made under provisions inserted into the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (c. 4) (“the 1992 Act”) by the Work and Families Act 2006 (c. 18), make provision relating to additional statutory paternity pay in respect of adoptions from overseas. An adoption from overseas is defined in the Regulations as an adoption of a child who enters Great Britain from outside the United Kingdom in connection with or for the purposes of adoption which does not involve the placement of the child for adoption under the law of any part of the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Employment Rights Act 1996 (Application of Section 80BB to Adoptions from Overseas) Regulations 2010
items.description: Section 80BB of the Employment Rights Act 1996 contains the powers under which the Secretary of State may make regulations entitling an employee adopting a child under the law of any part of the United Kingdom to additional paternity leave.

items.title: The Additional Paternity Leave (Adoptions from Overseas) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations implement the powers in the Work and Families Act 2006 to introduce a new entitlement for employees adopting children from overseas to take additional paternity leave in the first year after the child enters Great Britain. The provisions relating to additional paternity leave are incorporated by the Work and Families Act 2006 into the Employment Rights Act 1996 (“the 1996 Act”). SI 2010/1058 extends the application of the relevant provisions to apply to overseas adoptions.

items.title: The Infrastructure Planning (Fees) Regulations 2010
items.description: The Planning Act 2008 (“the Act”) provides for the granting of an order for development consent for development which is or forms part of a nationally significant infrastructure project. Development consent is required for certain types of energy, water, waste water, transport and waste projects. The Act also establishes the Infrastructure Planning Commission (“the Commission”) which is responsible for examining, and in most cases, determining applications for development consent. Section 4 of the Act enables the Secretary of State to make regulations for the charging of fees by the Commission in relation to the performance of any of its functions.

items.title: The Additional Statutory Paternity Pay (Weekly Rates) Regulations 2010
items.description: This instrument contains only regulations made by virtue of sections 10 and 11 of and paragraph 24 of Schedule 1 to the Work and Families Act 2006 (c.18), provisions which come into force on 6th April 2010. The instrument is made before the end of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of those provisions. The regulations in it are therefore exempted by section 173(5)(b) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c.5) from the requirement under section 172 of that Act to refer the proposals to make regulations to the Social Security Advisory Committee. They are therefore made without reference to the Committee.

items.title: The Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (Appropriate Officer and Schedule 7 Prescribed Persons) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations make provision relating to the further implementation of the Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (“the Act”) which establishes a new vetting and barring scheme in England and Wales.

items.title: The Public Guardian (Fees, etc.) (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Guardian (Fees. etc.) Regulations 2007 (SI 2007/2051) by inserting new paragraph (2) into regulation 10 (reductions and remissions in exceptional circumstances) providing that an application for the reduction or remission of any fee paid under regulation 8 (appointment of deputy: supervision fees) must be made to the Public Guardian within 6 months after the date of the invoice for that fee (regulation 3). The 6-month time limit applies only to applications for the reduction or remission of such fees, where the invoice for that fee is dated on or after 1st May 2010, the date of coming into force of these Regulations (regulation 4).

items.title: The Lasting Powers of Attorney, Enduring Powers of Attorney and Public Guardian (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations amend the Lasting Powers of Attorney, Enduring Powers of Attorney and Public Guardian Regulations 2007 (SI 2007/1253) (“the principal Regulations”). The amendments are as follows.

items.title: The Family Proceedings (Amendment) (No.2) Rules 2010
items.description: These Rules amend the Family Proceedings Rules 1991.

items.title: The Family Proceedings Courts (Children Act 1989) (Amendment) (No.2) Rules 2010
items.description: These Rules amend the Family Proceedings Court (Children Act 1989) Rules 1991.

items.title: The M60 Motorway (Junction 26 Clockwise Entry and Exit Slip Roads) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Crime and Disorder Act 1998 (Intervention Orders) (Amendment) Order 2010
items.description: This Order amends the Crime and Disorder Act 1998 (Intervention Orders) Order 2006 by prescribing the appropriate activities which may be included in an Intervention Order made under section 1G of the Crime and Disorder Act 1998. The appropriate activities are activities which are involved in the provision of services under the “Models of care for treatment of adult drug misusers: Update 2006” publication which can be downloaded on the National Treatment Agency for Substance Misuse’s website (www.nta.nhs.uk) or ordered by emailing nta@prolog.uk.com or telephoning 08701 555 455 and quoting product code MOC3.

items.title: The Health Act 2009 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2010
items.description: This Order brings into force in England provisions of sections 20, 21 and 22 of the Health Act 2009, which make amendments to earlier legislation in relation to tobacco. It also brings into force in England provisions of Schedule 4 to the Act (which makes minor and consequential amendments in relation to tobacco) and tobacco-related provisions of Schedule 6 to the Act (repeals and revocations).

items.title: The M5 Motorway (Junctions 9 - 12) (Temporary Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The Police Authority and Metropolitan Police Authority (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Authority Regulations 2008 (S.I. 2008/630) and the Metropolitan Police Authority Regulations 2008 (S.I. 2008/631) to provide that selection panels (which have the function of short-listing candidates for appointment as independent members of a police authority) may act with one fewer than their full complement of members if at least one week’s notice of the meeting in question has been given, if one member of the panel has died, or if a panel member has consented in writing to the other members acting in his absence. The Police Authority (Community Engagement and Membership) Regulations 2010 (S.I. 2010/421) reduced the number of members of selection panels to three (in the case of non-metropolitan police authorities) and four (in the case of the Metropolitan Police Authority) but omitted to make consequential changes to the numbers of members who could act without the full complement being present. These Regulations make such changes.

items.title: The Education (Short Stay Schools) (Closure) (England) Regulations 2010
items.description: Section 19 of the Education Act 1996 imposes a duty on local authorities to make arrangements for securing suitable education for children who, because of exclusion from school or for any other reason, may not receive such education if the arrangements are not made for them. Section 19(2B) recognises that local authorities may fulfil this duty by establishing and maintaining schools which are specifically organised to make educational provision for children falling within that section and where they do so, these schools are known as pupil referral units. Section 249(1) of the Apprenticeships, Skills, Children and Learning Act 2009 changes the name of any school falling within section 19(2B) of the Education Act 1996 from “pupil referral unit” to “short stay school”. It comes into force on 1 September 2010.

items.title: The Appointments Commission (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations amend the Appointments Commission Regulations 2006.

items.title: The Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (Supervisory Authorities and Devolution Alignment) Order 2010
items.description: This Order is made under the Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Education (Pupil Referral Units) (Application of Enactments) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: Section 19 of the Education Act 1996 requires local authorities to make arrangements for the provision of suitable education at school or otherwise than at school for those children of compulsory school age who, by reason of illness, exclusion from school or otherwise may not for any period receive suitable education unless such arrangements are made for them. Any school established and maintained by a local authority which is specially organised to provide education for such children is known as a pupil referral unit (section 19(2B) of the Education Act 1996). Schedule 1 to the Act provides that enactments which apply to maintained schools can be applied, adapted and modified by regulation to pupil referral units.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Technical Requirements for Inland Waterway Vessels) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations implement Directive 2006/87/EC dated 12 December 2006 of the European Parliament and of the Council laying down technical requirements for inland waterway vessels and repealing Council Directive 82/714/EEC (O.J. No. L 389, 30.12.2006, p.1) as amended by Directive 2006/137/EC dated 18 December 2006 of the European Parliament and of the Council (O.J. NO. L 389, 30.12.2006, p.261), Directive 2008/59/EC dated 12 June 2008 of the European Parliament and of the Council (O.J. No. L 166, 27.6.2008, p.31), Directive 2008/68/EC dated 24 September 2008 of the European Parliament and of the Council (O.J. No. L 260, 30.9.2008, p.13), Commission Directive 2008/87/EC dated 22 September 2008 (O.J. NO. L 255, 23.9.2008, p.5), Commission Directive 2008/126/EC dated 19 December 2008 (O.J. No. L 32, 31.1.2009, p.1) and Commission Directive 2009/46/EC dated 24 April 2009 (O.J. No. L 109, 30.4.2009, p.14).

items.title: The M6 Motorway (Junction 16, Southbound Exit Slip Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M6 Motorway (Junction 27 Northbound and Southbound Carriageways) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Parliamentary Elections (Welsh Forms) (Amendment) Order 2010
items.description: This Order amends the Parliamentary Elections (Welsh Forms) Order 2007 (S.I. 2007/1014) (“the 2007 Order”), which prescribes Welsh versions of certain forms or forms of words which may be used at United Kingdom parliamentary elections held in Wales. The English version of these forms are provided for in the Appendix of Forms to Schedule 1 to the Representation of the People Act 1983 (c.2).

items.title: The M56 Motorway Junctions 4-2 Eastbound Carriageway (Sharston Link) and Link Road and Slip Roads (Temporary Prohibition and Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The Poultry Compartments (England) Order 2010
items.description: This Order implements Commission Regulation EC (No) 616/2009 which makes provision for the recognition of poultry compartments which meet high standards of biosecurity (OJ No L 181, 14.7.2009, p 16). Since application for recognition is voluntary the only sanction for failure to comply with the requirements of this Order is removal of certification as a compartment.

items.title: The Apprenticeships, Skills, Children and Learning Act 2009 (Consequential Amendments) (England and Wales) Order 2010
items.description: Part 1 of Schedule 1 to the Order makes amendments that are consequential on the dissolution of the Learning and Skills Council for England (“the LSC”) by the Apprenticeships, Skills, Children and Learning Act 2009 (“the 2009 Act”). The LSC is dissolved by section 123(1) of the 2009 Act. Its functions in relation to education and training for those over compulsory school age are re-distributed, between local education authorities (Part 2 of the 2009 Act, in particular section 41), the new Young People’s Learning Agency for England (“the YPLA”) (Part 3 of that Act) and the new Chief Executive of Skills Funding (Part 4 of that Act). Schedule 6 to the 2009 Act makes some of the amendments to primary legislation needed as a result of this re-distribution of the LSC’s functions; the amendments in Part 1 of Schedule 1 to the Order are additional to those made by Schedule 6.

items.title: The M60 Motorway (Junction 13 Clockwise and Anticlockwise Slip Roads) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M6 Motorway (Junction 18 Northbound Carriageway and Junction 18 Northbound Slip Roads) (Temporary Prohibition and Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The A27 Trunk Road (Upper Brighton Road) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A27 Trunk Road (Hangleton Junction, Westbound) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Dairy (Specific Market Support Measure) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations provide for the distribution of monies in the United Kingdom for the purposes of Commission Regulation (EU) No 1233/2009 laying down a specific market support measure in the dairy sector (OJ No L 330, 16.12.2009, p. 70) (“the Commission Regulation”). They extend to the United Kingdom.

items.title: The A27 Trunk Road (Polegate) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Police Act 1997 (Criminal Records and Registration) (Jersey) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 12th April 2010, make detailed provision in relation to applications made in Jersey for criminal record certificates and enhanced criminal record certificates under Part 5 of the Police Act 1997 (“the Act”). These Regulations, which apply only in Jersey, mirror, with the appropriate modifications, the Regulations which apply in England and Wales. Part 2 is concerned with applications for criminal record and enhanced criminal record certificates. Part 3 is concerned with registration of persons under Part 5 of the Act.

items.title: The Transnational Information and Consultation of Employees (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 (c.68) and amend the Transnational Information and Consultation of Employees Regulations 1999 (SI 1999/3323) (as amended by SI 2004/1079, SI 2004/2326 as substituted by SI 2009/2401, SI 2004/2518 and SI 2006/2059) (the “1999 Regulations”). They implement Directive 2009/38/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 6th May 2009 on the establishment of a European Works Council or a procedure in Community-scale undertakings and Community-scale groups of undertakings for the purposes of informing and consulting employees (Recast) (OJ No L 122, 16.5.2009, p. 28); and Articles 7 and 8 of Directive 2008/104/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 19th November 2008 on temporary agency work (OJ No L327, 5.12.2008, p. 9) in so far as those Articles are relevant to the transnational information and consultation of employees.

items.title: The A303 Trunk Road (Weyhill - Andover) (Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2010
The South East Derbyshire College (Dissolution) Order 2010
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 15th February 2010 the further education corporation established to conduct The South East Derbyshire College. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to Derby College and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The Beef and Pig Carcase Classification (England) Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which apply in England only, revoke and remake the provisions of the Pig Carcase (Grading) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/2155) and the Beef Carcase (Classification) (England) Regulations 2004 (S.I. 2004/1317). The Regulations enforce Article 42 of, and Annex V to, Council Regulation (EC) No 1234/2007 (OJ No L 299, 16.11.2007, p 1), which relate to Community scales for the classification of carcases, and Commission Regulation (EC) No 1249/2008 (OJ No L 337, 16.12.2008, p 3), which sets out further details regarding the implementation of those scales for the classification of carcases. The Regulations relate to the carcases of adult bovine animals and pigs.

items.title: The Water Resources (Control of Pollution) (Silage, Slurry and Agricultural Fuel Oil) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: The Regulations amend the Water Resources (Control of Pollution) (Silage, Slurry and Agricultural Fuel Oil (England) Regulations 2010 (S.I. 2010/639), and correct a number of defects including clarifying the offence provisions in those Regulations. These Regulations are being issued free of charge to all known recipients of those Regulations.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 27 of the Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) Regulations 2002, which allows the Secretary of State to make available, for certain purposes, vehicle registration and licensing particulars kept on the vehicle register pursuant to section 21 of the Vehicle Excise and Registration Act 1994. The effect of the amendments is to permit disclosure of particulars to additional bodies for the purposes specified.

items.title: The Education and Skills Act 2008 (Commencement No. 6) Order 2010
items.description: This is the sixth commencement order made by the Secretary of State under the Education and Skills Act 2008 (“the Act”).

items.title: The End-of-Life Vehicles (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations amend the End-of-Life Vehicles Regulations 2003 (S.I.2003/2635) (“the 2003 Regulations”). The 2003 Regulations partially implement Directive 2000/53/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on end-of-life vehicles (OJ No L 269, 21.10.2000, p. 34) (“the ELV Directive”).

items.title: The End-of-Life Vehicles (Producer Responsibility) (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations amend the End-of-Life Vehicles (Producer Responsibility) Regulations 2005 (S.I. 2005/263) (“the 2005 Regulations”). The 2005 Regulations partially implement Directive 2000/53/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on end-of-life vehicles (OJ No L 269, 21.10.2000, p. 34).

items.title: The Ordnance Survey Trading Fund (Maximum Borrowing) Order 2010
items.description: The Ordnance Survey Trading Fund was established by the Ordnance Survey Trading Fund Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/965) on 1st April 1999.

items.title: The A19 Trunk Road and the A66 Trunk Road (Stockton Road Interchange) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (Commencement No. 6, Transitional Provisions and Savings (Amendment)) and (Commencement No. 7) Order 2010
items.description: This Order brings into force further provisions of the Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (“the Act”) and also makes amendments to transitional and savings provisions in the Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (Commencement No. 6, Transitional and Savings Provisions) Order 2009 (“the 2009 Order”).

items.title: The Flood Risk (Cross Border Areas) Regulations 2010
items.description: These regulations amend the Flood Risk Regulations 2009 and the Flood Risk Management (Scotland) Act 2009, both of which transpose Directive 2007/60/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the assessment and management of flood risks.

items.title: The M3 Motorway (Junction 6) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The RAF Welford Byelaws 2010
items.description: Part II of the Military Lands Act 1892 (c.43) provides for the making of byelaws for regulating the use of land held for military purposes and securing the safety of the public. These byelaws regulate the land at RAF Welford.

items.title: The Mesothelioma Lump Sum Payments (Conditions and Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations amend the Schedule to the Mesothelioma Lump Sum Payments (Conditions and Amounts) Regulations 2008 by substituting revised tables of lump sum payments payable to people with mesothelioma or to their dependants.

items.title: The Pneumoconiosis etc. (Workers’ Compensation) (Payment of Claims) (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: Under the Pneumoconiosis etc. (Workers’ Compensation) Act 1979 (c.41) (“the Act”) lump sum payments may be made to certain persons disabled by a disease to which the Act applies, or to dependants of persons who were so disabled before they died.

items.title: The Renewables Obligation (Amendment) Order 2010
items.description: This Order amends the Renewables Obligation Order 2009 (“the 2009 Order”) and makes transitional provision.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (Codes of Practice) (Revisions to Codes E and F) Order 2010
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1st May 2010 the revision of Code E (audio recording of interviews with suspects) to permit recording by secure digital network as an alternative to removable media format (audio tape). This follows on from a six month pilot scheme in the Eastern Division of Lancashire Constabulary (introduced by S.I. 2008/167) to trial use of this digital technology in interviews with suspects.

items.title: The Community Legal Service (Funding) (Amendment No. 2) Order 2010
items.description: This Order imposes conditions on the funding of services as part of the Community Legal Service, by limiting the powers of the Legal Services Commission to pay remuneration under contract for the provision of funded services. The Order substitutes a new schedule which sets out revised remuneration fees and rates which will be payable for funded services. The Order also requires the Commission to pay remuneration for advocacy services in certain family proceedings at rates and fees set out in a second schedule and in accordance with the 2010 Standard Civil Contract issued by the Commission. As a consequence of this, the Order governing the payment of remuneration to counsel in family proceedings is revoked.

items.title: The A249 Trunk Road (Key Street – Cowstead Roundabout) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Merchant Shipping and Fishing Vessels (Health and Safety at Work) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2010
items.description: Regulations 2 to 4 of these Regulations make minor drafting amendments to the Merchant Shipping and Fishing Vessels (Control of Noise at Work) Regulations 2007, the Merchant Shipping and Fishing Vessels (Control of Vibration at Work) Regulations 2007 and the Merchant Shipping and Fishing Vessels (Health and Safety at Work) (Carcinogens and Mutagens) Regulations 2007. The amendments clarify that those Regulations apply to hovercraft as they do to ships, and that any reference in those Regulations to the master of a ship means, in the case of a hovercraft, the captain of that hovercraft. Putting express provisions to this effect into these Regulations brings them into line with other Regulations relating to health and safety issues on merchant ships and fishing vessels.

items.title: The Vehicles Regulations (Amendment) Order 2010
items.description: This order amends four sets of Regulations to allow a United Kingdom identity card, issued under the Identity Cards Act 2006, to be used as an alternative to a passport in order to prove identity. The four sets of Regulations that are being amended cover (a) licence acquisition theory and practical driving tests (b) qualifying tests undertaken by driving instructors (c) initial qualification tests and periodic training undertaken by professional lorry and bus drivers to obtain or maintain their driver Certificate of Professional Competence and (d) the purchase of vehicle registration plates. In each of these cases a valid identity card will be accepted as proof of identity.

items.title: The M25 Motorway and the A2 and A282 Trunk Roads (Junctions 1B - 5) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A21 Trunk Road (Kipping’s Cross – Lamberhurst Quarter) (Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2010
The Motor Cycles Etc. and Tractors Etc. (EC Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends the Motor Cycles Etc. (EC Type Approval) Regulations 1999 in order to transpose Directive 2009/139/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Third Party Risks) (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: These regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Third Party Risks) Regulations 1972.

items.title: The Isles of Scilly (Children Act 1989) Order 2010
items.description: This Order is made under section 108(10) of the Children Act 1989 and comes into force on 26th April 2010.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Electronic Communication of Certificates of Insurance) Order 2010
items.description: Part 1 of this order amends the Road Traffic Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”). Part 2 of the order makes consequential amendments to the Motor Vehicles (Third Party Risks) Regulations 1972 (S.I. 1972/1217, “the 1972 regulations”).

items.title: The Legal Services Act 2007 (Commencement No. 7) Order 2010
items.description: This Order is the seventh commencement order made under the Legal Services Act 2007 (c. 29) (“the 2007 Act”).

items.title: The A27 Trunk Road (Bognor Road Roundabout – Stockbridge Roundabout, Westbound Carriageway) (Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2010
The Local Government and Public Involvement in Health Act 2007 (Commencement No.9) Order 2010
items.description: This Order commences provisions of the Local Government and Public Involvement in Health Act 2007 (c.28) (“the Act”) which concern byelaws in relation to local government and the duty to involve users in relation to health services.

items.title: The M25 Motorway (Junction 29, Anti-Clockwise Entry Slip Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M40 Motorway (Junctions 1 - 15) Slip Roads (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M2 Motorway (Medway Services, Londonbound) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A1(M) Motorway (Junction 46 to Junction 45) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M62 Motorway (Junction 29, Lofthouse Interchange) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M4 Motorway (Junction 20, Almondsbury Interchange) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A5111 Trunk Road (Alvaston, Derbyshire) (Closure of Gap in the Central Reservation) Order 2010
The M6 Motorway (Junction 4) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A46 Trunk Road (Newark-on-Trent) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic in Laybys) Order 2010
The M1 Motorway (Junction 22) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
THE A35 TRUNK ROAD (WINTERBOURNE ABBAS, DORSET) (RESTRICTION) ORDER 2000 (VARIATION) ORDER 2010
The M1 Motorway (Junctions 23 – 23a) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M1 Motorway (Junctions 21 – 22) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A14 Trunk Road (M1 Junction 19 to Portly Ford Bridge) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A30 Trunk Road (Alphington to Whiddon Down, Devon, and Launceston, Cornwall) (Temporary Prohibition and Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The Isles of Scilly (Functions) (Adoption and Children Act 2002) Order 2010
items.description: This Order is made under section 265(2) of the Local Government Act 1972 and comes into force on 26th April 2010.

items.title: The A35 Trunk Road (Puddletown to Bere Regis, Dorset) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The Medicines for Human Use (Miscellaneous Amendments) Order 2010
items.description: Articles 2 and 3 amend the Prescription Only Medicines (Human Use) Order 1997 (“the POM Order”) and the Medicines (Pharmacy and General Sale – Exemption) Order 1980 to include dental hygienists and dental therapists in the list of healthcare professionals that can sell, supply or administer medicines under a patient group direction.

items.title: The A30 Trunk Road (Tolvaddon Interchange to Scorrier Junction, Redruth, Cornwall) (Temporary Prohibition and Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The M4 Motorway (Junctions 18-17) (Temporary Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The A30 Trunk Road (Temple to Colliford Lake, Cornwall) (Temporary Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The M58 Motorway (Junctions 2-3 Eastbound Carriageway and Junction 3 Eastbound Exit Slip Road) (Temporary Prohibition and Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The Control of Artificial Optical Radiation at Work Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations implement as respects Great Britain Directive 2006/25/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council (O.J. L114, 27.4.2006, p.38) on the minimum health and safety requirements regarding the exposure of workers to risks arising from physical agents (artificial optical radiation) (nineteenth individual Directive within the meaning of Article 16(1) of Directive 89/391/EEC) (“the Directive”). The Regulations impose duties on employers to protect both employees who may be exposed to risk from exposure to artificial optical radiation at work and other persons at work who might be affected by that work.

items.title: The A14 Trunk Road (Between M1 Junction 19 and Thrapston) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic in Laybys) Order 2010
The Misuse of Drugs (Designation) (Amendment) (England, Wales and Scotland) Order 2010
items.description: Section 7(3) of the Misuse of Drugs Act 1971 (“the 1971 Act”) requires regulations to be made to allow the use for medical purposes of the drugs which are subject to control under the 1971 Act. Section 7(3) does not apply to any drug designated by order under section 7(4) as a drug to which section 7(4) is to apply. This Order amends the Misuse of Drugs (Designation) Order 2001 by inserting into Part 1 of the Schedule to that Order 4-methylmethcathinone (commonly referred to as mephedrone) and other cathinone derivatives.

items.title: The Misuse of Drugs (Amendment) (England, Wales and Scotland) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations insert 4–methylmethcathinone (commonly referred to as mephedrone) and other cathinone derivatives into Schedule 1 to the Misuse of Drugs Regulations 2001 (“the 2001 Regulations”). The schedule in which a controlled drug is placed primarily affects the extent to which the drug can be lawfully imported, exported, produced, supplied or possessed and dictates the record keeping, labelling and destruction requirements in relation to that drug.

items.title: The Pensions Act 2008 (Commencement No. 7 and Saving, Consequential and Incidental Provisions) Order 2010
items.description: This Order brings into force section 124(2) and (6) of the Pensions Act 2008 (c.30) (financial assistance scheme). These provisions make amendments in relation to the definition of “qualifying member” in section 286(2) of the Pensions Act 2004 (c.35). Section 124(2) and (6) were brought into force on 19th December 2008 for limited purposes. This Order brings those provisions into force for all remaining purposes from 31st March 2010.

items.title: The Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (Controlled Activity and Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations, made under the Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (“the Act”), make provision relating to controlled activity and to the criteria prescribed for the purposes of various paragraphs in Schedule 3 (barred lists) to the Act. The Regulations come into force on the day after the day on which they are made.

items.title: The Financial Assistance Scheme (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations amend the Financial Assistance Scheme Regulations 2005 (S.I. 2005/1986, as amended) (“the FAS Regulations”) which allow for payments to be made to, or in respect of, members or former members of certain occupational pension schemes where the liabilities of the scheme to those members have not been satisfied in full.

items.title: The Video Recordings (Labelling) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations, made under section 8 of the Video Recordings Act 1984 (“the 1984 Act”), specify certain requirements as to the indications of the contents of any classification certificate issued, in the form of labels and markings, and the position in which such labels and markings are to be shown on any video recording containing the video work in respect of which the certificate was issued. In addition, these Regulations specify the position in which such labels and markings are to be shown on any spool, case or other thing on or in which the video recording is kept.

items.title: The Cosmetic Products (Safety) (Amendment) Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Cosmetic Products (Safety) Regulations 2008 (S.I. 2008/1284) (“the Principal Regulations”) to give effect to Commission Directive 2009/129 of 9 October 2009 (OJ L267 of 10.10.2009, p18), Commission Directive 2009/130/EC of 12 October 2009 (OJ No L268, 13.10.2009 p.5), Commission Directive 2009/134/EC of 28 October 2009 (OJ No L282, 29.10.2009 p.15), Commission Directive 2009/164/EU of 22 December 2009 (OJ No L349 23.12.2009, p.41), as amended by Corrigendum (OJ No L3, 7.1.2010, p. 30), Commission Directive 2010/3/EU of 1 February 2010/3/EU (OJ No L29, p5) and Commission Directive 2010/4/EU (OJ No L36, 9.2.2010 p.21) (“the Directives”). The Directives amend Council Directive 76/768/EEC of 27 July 1976 (OJ No L262 27.9.1976 p169) on the approximation of the laws of the Member States on cosmetic products (“the Principal Directive”). The Principal Directive (as previously amended) has been implemented by the Principal Regulations.

items.title: The Apprenticeships, Skills, Children and Learning Act 2009 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional and Transitory Provisions) and (Commencement No. 2 (Amendment)) Order 2010
items.description: This Order brings into force provisions of the Apprenticeships, Skills, Children and Learning Act 2009 (“the Act”) on 1st April, 1st July and 1st September 2010 to the extent specified in column 2 of the appropriate tables.

items.title: The Representation of the People (Timing of the Canvass) (Northern Ireland) Order 2010
items.description: By virtue of section 10ZA of the Representation of the People Act 1983 (“the 1983 Act”), the Chief Electoral Officer for Northern Ireland is required to conduct a canvass in Northern Ireland in the year 2010. However, if, on or before 15 April 2010, the Chief Electoral Officer makes a recommendation to the Secretary of State against a canvass being conducted in 2010 for the purpose of meeting the relevant registration objectives (which are set out in section 10ZB of the 1983 Act), the Secretary of State can, by Order, make provision that that requirement shall not apply. Before doing so the Secretary of State must be satisfied, having considered the recommendation, that the public interest does not require a canvass to be conducted for that purpose.

items.title: The Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exceptions) (Amendment) (England and Wales) Order 2010
items.description: This Order amends the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exceptions) Order 1975 (“the Exceptions Order”).

items.title: The Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (Regulated Activity, Devolution and Miscellaneous Provisions) Order 2010
items.description: This Order amends the definition of regulated activity in the Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006 (“the Act”). It also makes amendments to the definitions of controlled activity in sections 21 and 22 of the Act and provides for the Independent Safeguarding Authority (“the ISA”) to be able to disclose information to police forces in Scotland and in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment (Amendment) Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment Regulations 2006 (SI 2006/3289) as amended by the Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment (Amendment) Regulations 2007 (SI 2007/3454) and the Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment (Amendment) Regulations 2009 (SI 2009/2957) (“the 2006 Regulations”). The 2006 Regulations transpose the main provisions of Council Directive 2002/96/EC of 27th January 2003 on waste electrical and electronic equipment (OJ No L37, 13.2.2002, p 24) as amended by Council Directive 2003/108/EC (OJ No L345, 31.12.2003, p 106) and Council Directive 2008/34/EC (OJ No L81, 20.3.2008, p 65) (“the WEEE Directive”).

items.title: The Education (Educational Provision for Improving Behaviour) Regulations 2010
items.description: The Education (Educational Provision for Improving Behaviour) Regulations 2010 apply to governing bodies of maintained schools in England. They impose requirements relating to the exercise of governing bodies’ powers under section 29A(1) of the Education Act 2002 to require a pupil to attend provision away from the school premises for the purpose of receiving education to improve the pupil’s behaviour (‘off-site provision’).

items.title: The Environmental Civil Sanctions (England) Order 2010
items.description: This Order is made under Part 3 of the Regulatory Enforcement and Sanctions Act 2008. It permits the Environment Agency and Natural England to impose civil sanctions in relation to the offences specified in Schedule 5 to the Order.

items.title: The Local Education Authorities and Children’s Services Authorities (Integration of Functions) Order 2010
items.description: This order repeals the definitions of “local education authority” in section 12 of the Education Act 1996 and the definitions of “children’s services authority in England” and “children’s services authority in Wales” in section 65(1) of the Children Act 2004. It then amends the definition of “local authority” in the Education Act 1996 and replaces it with new definitions (including separate definitions of “local authority in England” and “local authority in Wales”). It also inserts new definitions relating to “local authority” in the Children Act 2004 (again with separate definitions of “local authority in England” and “local authority in Wales).

items.title: The Environmental Civil Sanctions (Miscellaneous Amendments) (England) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations, which apply in England, amend—

items.title: The A55 Trunk Road (Junctions with A51, A483 and A41 Westbound and Eastbound Entry Slip Roads) (Temporary Prohibition and Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The Social Security (Loss of Benefit) Amendment Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Social Security (Loss of Benefit) Regulations 2001 (“the Loss of Benefit Regulations”) which are necessary as a result of the commencement of section 24 of the Welfare Reform Act 2009. That section amends the Social Security Fraud Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”) to provide for a new sanction for benefit offences under which there will be restrictions in payment of “sanctionable benefits” (which are most benefits except those relating solely to old age, disability and children). These restrictions will apply when a person has been convicted of one or more benefit offences in any proceedings, has agreed to pay an administrative penalty or has been cautioned for a benefit offence. The sanction may result in the total loss of benefit for four weeks or a reduction in benefit for four weeks. The new sanction will not apply where the offender has been convicted of one or more benefit offences in a set of proceedings where the offence was committed within the period of five years after the date on which the offender was convicted of one or more benefit offences in an earlier set of proceedings. The existing benefit sanction in section 7 of the 2001 Act will apply instead.

items.title: The Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment (Temporary Increase) Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations modify the Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment Regulations 2000 to provide for an increase in winter fuel payments for the 2010–11 winter.

items.title: The A46 Trunk Road (Whisby, Lincolnshire) (Temporary 40 Miles Per Hour Speed Restriction) Order 2010
The A52 Trunk Road (Nottingham) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A50 Trunk Road (Sawley Interchange, Leicestershire) (Eastbound Exit Slip Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The M1 Motorway (Junction 21, Leicestershire) (Southbound Exit Slip Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A36 Trunk Road (Black Dog Hill, Warminster, Wiltshire) (Layby Closure) (Experimental) Order 2010
The A1 Trunk Road (Cheswick to East Ord) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2010
The A12 Trunk Road (Junction 33, Copdock Mill Interchange, Suffolk to Junction 12, Marylands Interchange, Essex) (Prohibition of Entry) Order 2010
The A14 Trunk Road (Ipswich Western Bypass, Suffolk) (Prohibition of Entry) Order 2010

The M56 Motorway (Junctions 9-16, Westbound and Eastbound Carriageways, Link and Slip Roads), the M53 and M6 (Link Roads) and the A494 Trunk Road (Temporary Prohibition and  Restriction of Traffic) Order 2010
The A5 Trunk Road (High Street North, Dunstable, Bedfordshire) (Bus Lane) (No. 2) Order 2010
The Local Education Authorities and Children’s Services Authorities (Integration of Functions) (Local and Subordinate Legislation) Order 2010
items.description: This order follows on from the Local Education Authorities and Children’s Services Authorities (Integration of Functions) Order 2010 (S.I. 2010/1158).

items.title: The Children’s Centres (Inspections) Regulations 2010
items.description: These Regulations make provision relating to the inspection of children’s centres by Her Majesty’s Chief Inspector of Education, Children’s Services and Skills (“the Chief Inspector”). The Regulations are made under provisions in the Childcare Act 2006 which were inserted by the Apprenticeships, Skills, Children and Learning Act 2009, and they apply to England only.

items.title: The Designation of Schools Having a Religious Character (Independent Schools) (England) Order 2010
items.description: This Order designates the named independent schools as having a religious character.

items.title: The European Parliamentary Elections (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Regulations 2010 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the provision made in the European Parliamentary Elections (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2004 (“2004 Regulations”) for filling a vacancy arising mid-term in the seat of a member of the European Parliament for the Northern Ireland electoral region. A by-election will no longer always be held when a seat becomes vacant and how the vacancy is filled will depend on whether the previous member of the European Parliament (MEP) stood as an independent, in the name of a registered party, or in the name of two or more registered parties at the time she or he was elected.

items.title: The Ecclesiastical Exemption (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) (England) Order 2010
items.description: This Order is made under the Planning (Listed Building and Conservation Areas) Act 1990 (“the Act”) and revokes the Ecclesiastical Exemption (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) Order 1994 (SI 1994/1771) in respect of buildings situated in England.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) Order 2010
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, a safety zone having a radius of 500 metres from the specified point, around each installation specified in the Schedule to this Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c.29)).

items.title: The Electoral Law Act (Northern Ireland) 1962 (Amendment) Order 2010
items.description: The Electoral Law Act (Northern Ireland) 1962 (“the 1962 Act”) makes provision for local elections to district councils in Northern Ireland. In particular section 11 of that Act makes provision for filling casual vacancies that arise mid-term and Schedule 5 comprises the local elections rules (which govern the procedure by which local elections are conducted). This Order amends both section 11 and Schedule 5. It also makes consequential amendments to the Local Government Act (Northern Ireland) 1972, the Elected Authorities (Northern Ireland) Act 1989 and the Postponement of Local Elections (Northern Ireland) Order 2009.

items.title: The Concessionary Bus Travel Act 2007 (Variation of Reimbursement and Other Administrative Arrangements) Order 2010
items.description: This Order relates to the mandatory national concession which entitles older and disabled people to free off-peak travel on local bus services, and discretionary concessionary travel schemes which entitle certain categories of people to free or discounted local travel at the discretion of local authorities. Amendments of the Transport Act 2000 remove responsibility for administering the mandatory national concession from non-metropolitan district councils in England which are not unitary authorities and confer responsibility instead on county councils. Amendments of the Transport Act 1985 remove non-unitary and metropolitan district councils in England from the definition of ‘local authority’ in section 93(8) of that Act so they may no longer establish and administer discretionary concessionary travel schemes.

items.title: The Community Radio (Amendment) Order 2010
items.description: This Order, made under section 262 of the Communications Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”) amends the Community Radio Order 2004 (“the 2004 Order”), which had made various modifications to the Broadcasting Act 1990 (“the 1990 Act”) and the 2003 Act in relation to community radio.

items.title: The Identity Cards Act 2006 (Provision of Information with Consent) Regulations 2009 (Amendment) Regulations 2010
items.description: The Identity Cards Act 2006 (Provision of Information with Consent) Regulations 2009 were made on 23 September 2009 and came into force on 20 October 2009. There were some small errors in regulation 1(2) in relation to definitions. Those errors are corrected in these Regulations. Regulation 4 adds a person who is accredited by the Identity and Passport Service to be provided with information from passport records under the Passport Validation Service to the list of persons who may make an application under section 12(1)(a) and (b) and 12(6). Regulation 5 makes it clear that applications made by such persons are made under those sections only.

items.title: The Criminal Defence Service (Funding) (Amendment No. 2) Order 2010
